source: Pi session 2026-05-14 — Groq 429 'TPD: Limit 100000, Used 99432' after 228 turns
title: Normie mode must use a slim ~500-token consciousness, not the full ~30k-char root one
what_failed: Normie mode loaded the full root consciousness.txt (~30,819 chars, ~7-8k tokens) on every Groq call. After 228 chat turns the daily Groq TPD budget (100k tokens) was exhausted and normie became unusable for the rest of the day.
where_failed: agent/prompt.py::build_system_prompt_split — both modes shared the same consciousness body.
why_likely: Original design treated consciousness as universal. Normie was meant to be cheap chat but inherited root's prompt overhead, defeating the cost split.
suggested_fix: Add prompts/consciousness_normie.txt with the minimum needed for normie behaviour (identity, hard rules, refusal mapping). Wire build_system_prompt_split to load the slim version when mode='normie'. Cache the slim file in memory after first read.
